data.description:
Repro: open at least two workspaces in a sidebar; while viewing workspace A, click the X on background tab B (any state that triggers the close-confirm overlay — running process, etc.).

Observed: the confirm overlay appears (per the comment in TabManager.closeWorkspaceIfRunningProcess, anchored on the currently-selected workspace because the off-screen workspace's anchor view is hidden and reports no frame). The operator's view never switches to B, so the dialog references a workspace the operator isn't looking at.

Expected: clicking the X on a background tab should first select that tab (bring it into view), then present the close-confirm overlay on that tab. The overlay mounts on the workspace being closed.

Scope: single-workspace close path triggered by the sidebar X button. The multi-close path (multiple sidebar selection or Cmd+Shift+W with multi-select) is intentionally anchored on the currently-selected workspace and out of scope.

Touch points:
- Sources/TabManager.swift closeWorkspaceIfRunningProcess (requiresConfirmation branch; current 'host = selectedWorkspace ?? workspace' line)
- c11Tests/TabManagerUnitTests.swift — add a c11LogicTests-safe unit test asserting selection switches to the workspace being closed before the confirmation handler fires.
data.title: Sidebar X on a background tab: focus that tab before showing the close-confirm overlay

data.body:
PR: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/206

Fix: closeWorkspaceIfRunningProcess in Sources/TabManager.swift now selects the target workspace before presenting the close-confirm overlay if it isn't already the visible one. The old 'host = selectedWorkspace ?? workspace' indirection collapses away. Scoped to the single-workspace close path; the multi-close summary path is unchanged.

Tests: c11-logic 899/899 green locally; c11Tests + c11LogicTests build clean. Two new unit tests added to TabManagerUnitTests (host-bound, validated in CI per local-test policy).

provenance.reason: PR open, awaiting CI + human review

data.body: Operator-validated in c11-117 tagged dev build (after follow-up commit accd9f4 covering the Bonsplit pane-tab close path — the original TabManager-only commit fixed sidebar workspace tabs but not the in-pane tab strip, which is what the operator was actually hitting). Both close-confirm paths now focus the target tab before the dialog appears.
Operator-validated against c11-117 tagged dev build: clicking X on a background pane-tab brings it to the front and surfaces the close-confirm card on it. Approved for merge.
